headline: US online spending hits $8.3 billion as Amazon Prime Day kicks off, Adobe says
summary: Online spending among shoppers on the first day of Amazon's Prime ‌Day rose 5.3% from a year earlier ‌to $8.3 billion across U.S. retailers, Adobe Analytics said on ​Wednesday.  The four-day shopping event, which started on Tuesday and is being held earlier than usual this year, will be a litmus test of U.S. ‌shoppers' spending power ⁠as the focus shifts to essential goods.  • Online ⁠spending on Tuesday is tracking ahead of its projections and marks the biggest e-commerce day so ​far in ​2026, Adobe said.
